title: 10 best herbal creams for eye swelling

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation and damage, promoting healthy vision and reducing oxidative stress.

Moisturizing and soothing effects: Herbal aloe creams' moisturizing and soothing effects help calm irritated eyes and reduce swelling by hydrating the skin and eyelids, while also providing a protective barrier against environmental irritants.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce swelling and redness. When applied topically, aloe vera cream can calm the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness and swelling. Its hydrating properties also help to lock in moisture, leaving the skin feeling cool and refreshed. Regular use can lead to a more radiant and youthful appearance.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds, particularly aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce eye swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Polysaccharides: The polysaccharide component of Aloe vera, particularly acemannan, assists in reducing eye swelling by promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and modulating the immune response.

Glucomannans: Glucomannans in Aloe vera help reduce eye swelling by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, and reducing vascular permeability.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain gel, which is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds and soothing properties, making it ideal for reducing eye swelling.
Leaves (Gel): The gel in aloe vera leaves is known for its hydrating and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce eye puffiness and swelling.
Leaves (Extracts): Aloe vera leaf extracts are often used in creams for their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate eye swelling and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried aloe vera gel powder into a fine consistency using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the aloe vera powder with the beeswax mixture and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a bowl.
Add 2 tablespoons of distilled water to the mixture and stir well until a smooth consistency is achieved.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Witch Hazel contains tannins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidants: The high antioxidant content in Witch Hazel helps protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to puffiness and swelling.
Astringent properties: Witch Hazel's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, making it an effective remedy for reducing eye swelling and puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana, also known as American witch hazel, creams helps with eye swelling because of its powerful anti-inflammatory properties. The extracts of Hamamelis virginiana contain tannins and flavonoids that reduce puffiness and redness by constricting blood vessels and soothing the skin. These natural compounds also possess antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and promote healing. As a result, applying a Hamamelis virginiana cream to the affected area can provide quick relief from eye swelling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins in Hamamelis virginiana help with eye swelling by reducing inflammation and constricting blood vessels, thereby decreasing the swelling and redness associated with eye puffiness.

Gallic acid: Gallic acid, a polyphenolic compound present in Hamamelis virginiana, helps with eye swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the swelling and promote the healing of the affected area.

Hamamelitannin: Hamamelitannin, a complex tannin found in Hamamelis virginiana, helps with eye swelling by exerting astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the swelling and prevent further irritation of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hamamelitannin, a compound that provides anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making it effective in reducing eye swelling.
Barks: Contains tannins that have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, helping to alleviate eye swelling and discomfort.
Leaves: Rich in hamamelitannin and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, useful in soothing eye swelling and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in the spring or early summer when they are fully open.
Combine the dried plant material with 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every few days to ensure even extraction.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold creams contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness around the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams help to neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye swelling and inflammation, promoting a more even and healthy appearance.
Soften and soothe skin: Pot marigold contains beta-carotene, which helps to soften and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. When applied topically, Calendula cream can help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and discomfort. Its natural properties also help to combat free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling. Regular use may provide relief from eye swelling.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce eye swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which alleviate inflammation and oxidative stress associated with swelling.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Calendula officinalis creams have been found to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antiedematous activities, which help in reducing swelling and edema around the eyes.

Saponins: Saponins present in the Calendula officinalis plant exhibit anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties, which aid in reducing the severity of eye swelling by suppressing the release of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds, making them ideal for reducing eye swelling.
Leaves: They contain similar properties to flowers, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which help to reduce eye swelling and promote healing.

(Optional, but sometimes used) Stems: They can be used to produce extracts that contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can aid in reducing eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried calendula officinalis flowers from a sunny location for optimal potency.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Kansas coneflower may help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye swelling by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidant activity: The plant's antioxidant properties could help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling.

Immune-modulating effects: Some studies suggest that Kansas coneflower extracts may have immunomodulatory effects, potentially helping to reduce the body's inflammatory response and alleviate eye swelling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream, typically derived from the root of the plant, contains compounds that reduce swelling and ease discomfort. When applied topically to the affected area, the cream's anti-inflammatory agents work to calm the skin and promote a reduction in puffiness. This natural remedy is often used to alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or other irritants, offering a soothing and effective solution.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Alkaloids (specifically, echinacoside): Echinacoside has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate eye swelling by reducing the production of inflammatory mediators.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Echinacea angustifolia, exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to reduce eye swelling and protect against oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, particularly echinacoside and echinulin, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their flavonoid and phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their flavonoids and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that help alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder at low speed.
Mix the echinacea powder with a carrier oil such as coconut oil in a 1:10 ratio by weight and add a preservative like vitamin e.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer or whisk until it thickens to a smooth and spreadable cream consistency in 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds and scavenging free radicals.

Berberidin: Berberidin, a flavonoid present in goldenseal, has been shown to have potent anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in reducing eye swelling caused by allergic reactions or inflammation.

Cinchona alkaloids: Cinchona alkaloids, including quinine and quinidine, have vasoconstrictive properties that can help reduce blood flow to the affected area, thereby decreasing edema (swelling) and promoting faster healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The berberine content in goldenseal has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling, while its antimicrobial properties help to combat infections that can cause eye swelling. Additionally, goldenseal's ability to improve blood flow and reduce fluid buildup in the eyes helps to quickly alleviate swelling, making it a popular natural remedy for puffy eyes and eyelids. This natural cream provides a gentle and soothing solution for eye care.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine, an alkaloid compound, helps with eye swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and redness caused by inflammation.
Hydrastine: Hydrastine, an alkaloid compound, helps with eye swelling by exerting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing the severity of eye swelling and promoting healing.

Vasophore: Vasophore, a phenolic compound, helps with eye swelling by exhibiting vasoconstrictive properties, which helps in reducing blood flow to the affected area and subsequently reducing swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of berberine, a key compound responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory properties, which helps alleviate eye swelling.
Leaves: They contain berberine and hydrastine, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful in reducing eye swelling.
Rhyzomes: They contain a combination of berberine and hydrastine, which, when applied topically, help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with eye conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 roots of hydrastis canadensis, carefully selecting those with a rich golden color and no signs of decay.
Clean the roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft-bristled brush under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and then infuse them in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding the solids and retaining 1 cup of liquid.
Combine the retained liquid with 1/2 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of beeswax, then heat the mixture over low heat for 5-7 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, help reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness around the eyes by blocking pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, including flavonoids and polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye swelling, promoting a healthier eye environment.

Vasodilatory: Dandelion's vasodilatory properties help improve blood circulation around the eyes, reducing fluid buildup and swelling by increasing the removal of excess fluids and waste products through lymphatic drainage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The key compound present in the plant, taraxasterol, has been found to reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce puffiness and discomfort associated with eye swelling, making it a popular natural remedy for treating puffy eyes, dark circles, and other eye-related issues.
remedy_constituents: Luteolin: A flavonoid antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin that exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help alleviate eye swelling and redness by reducing the activity of inflammatory cells.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce eye swelling by inhibiting the release of histamine and other pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used to make creams for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make creams for eye swelling due to their ability to reduce swelling and inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make creams for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh leaves and flowers of taraxacum officinale on a sunny morning in spring.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Infuse 1 cup of dried taraxacum officinale in 2 cups of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Mix 1/2 cup of infused taraxacum officinale with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and forms a smooth cream, taking about 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Plantain has antioxidant properties that help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Soothing and cooling properties: The mucilages present in plantain can provide a soothing and cooling effect on the eyes, helping to reduce puffiness, redness, and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plant contains compounds that reduce redness and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, plantain has been traditionally used to calm irritated skin and tissues, which makes it effective in reducing puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties provide quick relief and help to alleviate eye swelling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Aucubin: Aucubin is a phenylethanoid glycoside that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes.
Plantaginic acid: Plantaginic acid is a phenolic acid that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate eye swelling and promote healing.

Bisbenzylisoquinoline alkaloids (e.g., aucubine): These alkaloids have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye swelling and promote overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes.
Seeds: They are used because of their potential to reduce swelling and inflammation, as well as their ability to promote the healing of wounds and soothe irritated skin.
Roots: They are used for their purported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate eye swelling and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 20 grams of fresh plantago major leaves on a dry sunny day.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours.
Steep one teaspoon of dried plantago major leaves in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion and combine it with 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes until it thickens and forms a smooth creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as salicylic acid and flavonoids, help reduce puffiness and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: Nettle cream's antioxidant agents, like polyphenols and carotenoids, combat oxidative stress and free radicals that can contribute to eye swelling, promoting overall eye health and reducing inflammation.

Vasodilatory properties: The vasodilating compounds in stinging nettle cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help dilate blood vessels around the eyes, improving blood flow and reducing swelling by reducing fluid buildup.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, help to reduce inflammation and swelling by blocking the release of chemical mediators responsible for inflammation. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing properties help to calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. This makes Urtica dioica cream a popular natural remedy for alleviating eye swelling and promoting overall eye health. Its effectiveness is due to its ability to address the root causes of inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and chemicals.
Fisetin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate eye swelling and redness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
Apigenin: A flavonoid that acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the eyes by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Urtica dioica leaves are commonly used due to their high content of histamine-releasing compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and swelling.
Stems: Urtica dioica stems contain anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins and polyphenols that help soothe and calm the skin, making them a popular choice for eye cream formulations.

Roots: Urtica dioica roots are used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds, including glycosides and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the urtica dioica plant leaves and stems from a trusted source in late spring or early summer when in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 105°f for 2 hours or air-dry for 7 days.
Combine 1 cup of dried urtica dioica plant material with 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in chamomile cream neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye swelling, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing inflammation.
Sothining and calming effects: Chamomile's soothing and calming properties help reduce eye irritation and discomfort by relaxing the muscles around the eyes and reducing stress-induced inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have a calming effect on the skin, reducing redness and puffiness. The cream's cooling sensation also helps to constrict blood vessels, further reducing swelling. Additionally, chamomile's antihistamine properties can help alleviate allergic reactions that may contribute to eye swelling, making it a popular natural remedy for this common issue.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate eye irritation.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing swelling and promoting healing.
Alpha-bisabolol acetate: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and protect the eyes from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce eye swelling.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing eye swelling.
Seeds: Rich in essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak potency and store them in airtight containers.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a double boiler to obtain 100g of essential oil.
Combine 200g of distilled water with 1g of sodium lauryl sulfate in a glass beaker.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of candelilla wax and heat the mixture in a double boiler.
Blend the essential oil with the wax mixture and add it to the sodium lauryl sulfate solution to create the cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and redness.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from oxidative damage, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Soothing and calming effects: The gentle, calming properties of lavender cream can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to eye swelling, allowing the eyes to relax and recover more effectively.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. When applied topically, these creams can help to calm and relax the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and promoting a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance. This makes English lavender creams a popular natural remedy for relieving eye swelling and promoting a more radiant complexion.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool, a terpene found in Lavandula angustifolia, helps with eye swelling by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which in turn eases swelling and discomfort.

Linalyl acetate: Linalyl acetate, another terpene in Lavandula angustifolia, aids in reducing eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and promoting vasodilation, which helps to reduce swelling and promote blood flow.

Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid found in Lavandula angustifolia, helps with eye swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce inflammation and protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with eye conditions.
Flowers: Rich in antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing and calming the eye area.
Stems: Contain essential oils that help to relax and reduce puffiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom in the early morning.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on a wire rack in a warm place for 1 week.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried flowers, 1/2 cup of distilled water, and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, then steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean bowl and whip in 1/4 cup of cocoa butter until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut creams helps with eye swelling because they contain aescin, a powerful flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties. Aescin reduces fluid buildup in the eyes by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in inflammation, thereby decreasing swelling and puffiness. Additionally, horse chestnut extracts have been shown to improve blood circulation around the eyes, further reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness. By targeting both inflammation and circulation, herbal horse chestnut creams provide effective relief from eye swelling and related cosmetic concerns.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin is a saponin compound found in horse chestnut that helps to reduce inflammation and edema (swelling) by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory agents and improving blood vessel permeability, which alleviates puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in horse chestnut creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, reducing the appearance of swelling and discoloration.

Saponins: Saponins, another type of compound found in horse chestnut, exhibit vasoconstrictive effects, causing blood vessels to constrict or narrow, which helps to reduce fluid accumulation and swelling around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and relieving discomfort associated with eye swelling.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and promote blood circulation, alleviating eye swelling symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50g of dried aesculus hippocastanum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered aesculus hippocastanum bark with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 20 drops of chamomile essential oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients are fully incorporated and the oil has a warm, slightly thickened consistency.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or use immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with eye swelling because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and congestion. The cream's active ingredients, derived from the Maidenhair tree's leaves, have a soothing effect on the skin and eyes, gently calming puffiness and redness. As the cream is absorbed, it also cools and relaxes the surrounding tissues, further reducing swelling and discomfort. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for alleviating eye swelling, leaving eyes looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which may contribute to swelling.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may improve blood flow to the eyes, reducing swelling and inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and pain in the eyes by inhibiting pro-inflammatory molecules.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye swelling.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in the shade for 24 hours.
Grind 50g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20g of beeswax, 10g of coconut oil, and 5g of ginkgo bilboa powder in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 60°c before whipping until thickened for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry creams helps with eye swelling because they are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The anthocyanins and polyphenols present in blueberries have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid retention. Additionally, the soothing properties of these creams can calm irritated eyes and provide long-lasting relief from eye swelling, making them an effective natural remedy for reducing puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Blueberries are rich in antioxidants such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thereby reducing swelling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal blueberry creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid and quercetin, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with eye inflammation.
Moisturizing properties: The emollient properties of blueberries can help to soothe and hydrate the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation that may contribute to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the blueberry plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and puffiness around the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the blueberry plant contain antioxidants and fatty acids, which help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus leaves and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
Combine 100g of dried vaccinium myrtillus leaves with 500ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat for 3-4 hours or until it reaches a temperature of 70°c to 80°c.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The silymarin in milk thistle has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating discomfort and promoting a sense of calm around the delicate eye area. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties moisturize and soothe the skin, further reducing the appearance of swelling and leaving eyes looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: This flavonoid compound has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm irritated or inflamed tissues around the eyes, reducing swelling.

Lectins: Some herbal milk thistle creams contain lectins, such as silybin, which have been shown to have a mild vasoconstrictive effect, helping to reduce blood flow and thereby alleviate congestion and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and antioxidants that promote skin health and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the plant material using a dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or air drying.
Grind 20g of dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 5g of the powdered herb with 10g of beeswax and 15g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with eye swelling because of their unique composition. The mucilages in these creams create a soothing, protective barrier on the skin that reduces inflammation and irritation. This allows the eyes to calm down and return to their normal state, reducing the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, marshmallow creams' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the underlying causes of swelling, providing long-lasting relief for dry, tired, or irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can reduce inflammation and swelling around the eyes.
Moisturizing: The mucilages present in marshmallow root have excellent moisturizing properties, helping to soothe and hydrate dry, irritated eyes and reduce puffiness.

Antioxidant: Marshmallow root contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing swelling and redness.
Buds: Containing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, they help to reduce puffiness and discomfort caused by swollen eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry althaea officinalis leaves and flowers to ensure optimal potency for subsequent use.
Combine 200g of dried althaea officinalis with 400ml of organic coconut oil in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 30 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Add 10ml of beeswax and stir until fully incorporated before pouring the mixture into sterilized containers.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature over 2 hours before use or storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like citral and limonene, which have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in various parts of the body, including the eyes. When applied topically as a cream, lemon verbena can help to soothe and calm irritated or inflamed eyes, reducing puffiness and promoting a more refreshed and radiant appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lemon verbena, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye swelling and damage, promoting healthy tissue and reducing oxidative stress.

Vasodilatory properties: Lemon verbena's ability to dilate blood vessels (vasodilation) helps improve blood flow to the eyes, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling by removing excess fluid and promoting drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and relieve itching around the eyes.
Buds: The buds contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly.
Steep 20g of dried verbena officinalis in 100ml of hot distilled water for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add 30% beeswax to the liquid and heat it gently until fully incorporated and melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely before transferring to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams help with eye swelling because they contain high levels of silica, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces puffiness and irritation. The cream's soothing properties calm the delicate skin around the eyes, while the antioxidant-rich formula neutralizes free radicals that can contribute to inflammation. As a result, herbal field horsetail creams provide fast and effective relief from eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or other irritants, leaving your peepers feeling cool, calm, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes by reducing inflammation, making it an effective remedy for puffy or swollen eyes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in horsetail cream help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling and dark circles, promoting a smoother, more even appearance under the eyes.
Astringent: Horsetail's astringent properties help constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid retention and edema around the eyes, which can further exacerbate puffiness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce eye swelling by calming down inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Contain high levels of silica, which helps to strengthen blood vessels and reduce edema in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality equisetum arvense plant material of suitable size and quantity for preparation.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or desiccators within 24 hours of collection.
Grind the dried equisetum arvense into a fine powder weighing 200 grams to ensure uniform extraction.
Steep 50 grams of ground equisetum arvense in 500 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil for 2 weeks under room temperature conditions.
Strain and filter the infused oil mixture, then mix it with beeswax and sweet almond oil in a 1:1 ratio to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with eye swelling because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The active ingredients in fennel, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have a natural ability to calm and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing water retention and constriction of blood vessels. As a result, herbal fennel creams can help to alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or tiredness, leaving the eyes feeling refreshed, smooth, and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel contains anti-inflammatory compounds like anethole, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant activity: Fennel's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye swelling, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health.

Vasodilatory effects: Fennel's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels in the eyes, which improves circulation and reduces swelling by allowing excess fluid and waste products to be removed more effectively.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing eye swelling and discomfort.
Buds: Fennel buds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to soothe and calm inflamed eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and dry them thoroughly in shade for at least two hours before use.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for seven days, shaking occasionally, then strain through cheesecloth and discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with 30 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut butter in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined.
Pour the warm cream into clean glass containers, allow to cool and set at room temperature for at least two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with eye swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol present in peppermint oil reduces puffiness and soothes irritated eyes by constricting blood vessels and decreasing fluid accumulation. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation provides instant relief from itching and burning sensations associated with swollen eyes. As a result, applying herbal peppermint creams can effectively alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or dry eyes, promoting a clearer and more comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and swelling in the eyes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The menthol and methyl salicylate present in peppermint creams have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Cicatrizant properties: Peppermint's cicatrizant compounds, such as flavonoids, can promote collagen synthesis and improve tissue repair, which may help reduce the appearance of dark circles and puffiness under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Leaves: Fresh peppermint leaves contain menthol, which helps to reduce redness and itchiness associated with swollen eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants.
Steam distill the peppermint leaves using a clevenger apparatus for at least three hours to extract the essential oil.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax with 40 grams of coconut oil and 30 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl, stirring until fully incorporated.
Add 2 milliliters of the peppermint essential oil to the melted wax mixture, stirring well to combine, and allow it to cool for five minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterile glass jars and allow it to solidify at room temperature over a period of one hour before using as an eye cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The cream's active ingredients, such as eucalyptus and peppermint oils, work to reduce puffiness and redness by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation in the eyes. This natural remedy provides fast and gentle relief from eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or other environmental factors, allowing for a more comfortable and clear visual experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptus globulus oil contains compounds like eucalyptol and globulol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce swelling in the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: The oil has antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to inflammation and swelling.
Soothing and calming effects: Eucalyptus globulus oil has a calming and soothing effect on the skin, which could potentially help reduce redness and discomfort associated with eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
Roots: The roots of the plant have been traditionally used to treat various eye ailments, including swelling, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 20% eucalyptus globulus essential oil, 60% coconut oil, and 20% beeswax.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for approximately 10-15 minutes.
Add the coconut oil to the melted beeswax and stir until fully incorporated for about 5 minutes.
Combine the eucalyptus globulus essential oil with the melted coconut oil mixture and stir well for approximately 2 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized containers and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for eye swelling are topical creams made from natural herbs that help reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes. These creams contain anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that alleviate eye swelling, making them a popular alternative to harsh chemical-based treatments. The best herbal creams for eye swelling include Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Hamamelis virginiana, Arnica montana, Echinacea purpurea, Symphytum officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, eye swelling can cause permanent damage, disrupt sleep patterns, and lead to vision problems, severely impacting one's quality of life and daily activities.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye swelling that are treated with herbal creams are various skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil help soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing redness and itching. Other conditions like rosacea, acne, and minor burns can also be treated with herbal creams to promote healing and reduce discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with eye swelling is a blend of arnica, calendula, and chamomile. Arnica cream reduces inflammation and soothes the skin, while calendula cream promotes wound healing and tissue repair. Chamomile cream calms the skin and reduces redness, making it an effective combination for eye swelling caused by allergies, irritations, or minor injuries. Apply a thin layer of this mixture to the affected area several times a day for optimal results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin. When applied topically to the affected area, Calendula creams can help to calm and comfort the eyes, reducing redness and swelling. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from eye puffiness and discomfort, making it a popular choice for those seeking a gentle and non-invasive solution.
remedy_constituents: Neryl acetate: Helps reduce eye swelling by its anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.

Triterpenoids (specifically, oleanolic acid and ursolic acid): Assist in reducing eye swelling by exerting potent anti-inflammatory effects through the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Quercetin: Contributes to the reduction of eye swelling by its ability to scavenge free radicals, inhibit the activity of inflammatory enzymes, and suppress the expression of pro-inflammatory genes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Calendula officinalis in creams for eye swelling due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds.
Leaves: The leaves contain similar properties to the flowers and are often used in combination to enhance the effectiveness of the creams.
Seeds: The seeds are used to extract oil, which is then incorporated into creams to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried calendula officinalis flowers and infuse them in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Combine 1/4 cup of the calendula infusion with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax and coconut oil have melted and combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which reduce inflammation and swelling. Aloe vera also has antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals and prevent further damage to the skin. As a result, it can help to reduce puffiness and redness, providing immediate relief from eye swelling. This natural remedy can be applied topically to the affected area to provide quick and effective results.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Aloin: Aloin is a potent anti-inflammatory and anti-swelling agent that helps reduce edema and redness in the eyes by blocking the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.

Glucomannan: Glucomannan is a polysaccharide with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that helps reduce swelling in the eyes by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the skin and reducing water retention.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Gel from Leaves: The aloe vera gel extracted from the leaves helps to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Mucilage from Leaves: The mucilage found in the leaves has a cooling effect that helps to reduce swelling and discomfort around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe barbadensis gel by cutting off outer leaves and scooping out the inner pulp weighing 200g.
Combine 100g of distilled water with 10g of gel in a blender and blend for 5 minutes at medium speed.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl to remove any remaining pulp and fibers.
Add 10g of cetearyl olivate and 10g of sorbitan olivate to the strained mixture and mix until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and store in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, tannins, reduces blood flow to the affected area, thereby reducing puffiness and swelling. The cooling sensation of the cream also provides immediate relief from irritation and discomfort. Additionally, Hamamelis virginiana has natural antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, making it an effective and natural remedy for eye swelling and puffiness. Its gentle and non-irritating nature makes it suitable for sensitive skin.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce eye swelling by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, thereby alleviating inflammation and promoting healing.
Tannins: Assist in reducing eye swelling by contracting blood vessels, thus decreasing fluid accumulation and edema in the affected area.
Phenolic acids: Contribute to reducing eye swelling by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to mitigate the underlying causes of swelling and promote tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory and astringent compounds, making them effective in reducing eye swelling.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area.
Leaves: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 4 ounces of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 8 ounces of distilled water with 4 ounces of a mixture of beeswax and candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Add the hamamelis virginiana liquid to the wax mixture and stir until the mixture is smooth and emulsified.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 to 40 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from the plant's bioactive compounds. These compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, work to reduce swelling and ease pain. When applied topically, Arnica creams can help to reduce the inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. This makes Arnica a popular natural remedy for treating eye swelling caused by injuries, allergies, or other factors. Its soothing effects can also help to calm irritated skin and promote a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain around the eyes.

Sesquiterpene lactones (specifically, helenalin and dihydrohelenalin): These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and anti-edematous effects, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort around the eyes.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic and ferulic acids): These plant acids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and promote healing around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye swelling.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to absorb and transfer active compounds from the flowers, making them a valuable addition to eye swelling creams.
Stems: They are used due to their cell walls, which are rich in helenalin and other anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate eye swelling and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 arnica montana flowers in their full bloom stage for maximum potency.
Dry the arnica flowers at 35°c for 24 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind 20 grams of dried arnica flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler for 5 minutes.
Combine the arnica powder with the melted wax and oil mixture and stir for 2 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and puffiness. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, work together to soothe and calm the affected area. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to possess antioxidant properties, helping to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from further damage. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from eye swelling, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic solution.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Phenolic acids: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing eye swelling.
Alkylamides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help to regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the eyes, thereby alleviating swelling.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make creams for eye swelling because they are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, which help reduce swelling and soothe the skin.

Roots: The roots are used to make creams for eye swelling because they contain a high concentration of polysaccharides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate eye swelling.

Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for eye swelling because they are a good source of flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce eye swelling and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency and freshness.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers thoroughly in a warm oven at 150°f for 1-2 hours.
Infuse 2 cups of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens into a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with eye swelling because they contain compounds such as allantoin and mucilages, which possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and calm the skin, thereby alleviating the swelling and discomfort associated with eye puffiness. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the skin, reducing the appearance of swelling and promoting a more even tone. As a result, Symphytum officinale creams can provide effective relief from eye swelling and promote a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids in comfrey help with eye swelling by reducing inflammation and pain through their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Sterols: Sterols in comfrey help with eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing the severity of inflammation.
Triterpenoid saponins: Triterpenoid saponins in comfrey help with eye swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, thereby reducing swelling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to reduce eye swelling.
Roots: Used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate pain and swelling associated with eye conditions.
Stems: (Not widely reported, but possible) - Not widely reported as part of creams, however, they do contain mucilages that are used in creams for skin and eye soothing purposes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of the dried roots and leaves of symphytum officinale in the summer months.
Infuse 500 milliliters of a carrier oil like sweet almond oil with the dried plant material for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 100 grams of the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler and allow it to cool and thicken before transferring to jars.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with eye swelling because they possess anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the licorice root. The key compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the skin. When applied topically, it helps to soothe puffy eyes and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, further reducing the appearance of swelling and promoting a more refreshed and revitalized look.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help reduce eye swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties and inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Liquiritin: Liquiritin shows anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce eye swelling by modulating the production of inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.

Glycyrrhetic acid: Glycyrrhetic acid exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties, which contribute to its ability to alleviate eye swelling caused by allergic reactions or irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation and swelling.
Barks: Used due to their ability to provide anti-inflammatory properties and soothe irritated skin.
Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and wash them thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities.
Steep the roots in 500 milliliters of hot distilled water for 30 minutes to 1 hour to extract active compounds.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the roots and obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 100 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil with 20 milliliters of the herbal extract and stir well to combine the ingredients.
Whip 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of candelilla wax in a double boiler for 5-10 minutes to create a smooth cream base.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce blood pressure. The quinine content in the cream helps to constrict blood vessels, which in turn reduces the amount of fluid accumulation in the eye area. This results in a decrease in swelling and puffiness. Additionally, the natural cooling effect of the cream provides relief from the discomfort associated with eye swelling, making it a popular remedy for conditions such as puffy eyes and edema.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with eye swelling.
Quinidine: Quinidine possesses vasoconstrictive properties, which can help constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Quinic acid: Quinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and reduce inflammation that may contribute to eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona officinalis are used to make creams for eye swelling due to their high content of quinine and other alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis are used to make creams for eye swelling due to their ability to reduce swelling and inflammation caused by allergies or minor injuries.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried cinchona officinalis bark from a reputable supplier with a known source.
Combine the dried cinchona officinalis bark with 200ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 70-80°c to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar to separate the solids.
Add 2% preservative and 2% emulsifier to the oil and mix well until fully incorporated.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with eye swelling because they contain a high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin, potent anti-inflammatory compounds found in St. John's Wort. These ingredients have been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in the skin, making them effective in alleviating eye puffiness and swelling. The creams also possess antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and promote healthy skin cell regeneration, further contributing to their anti-inflammatory effects. This leads to a reduction in eye swelling and a more refreshed appearance.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Quercetin: A type of flavonoid, quercetin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from oxidative damage.

Naphthodianthrones: Specifically hyperforin and hypericin, these compounds exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to alleviate swelling, reduce pain, and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce eye swelling.
Flowers: They are a good source of flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help alleviate eye swelling.
Stems: They contain hyperforin and hypericin, which are responsible for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 60 grams of base cream containing 20% shea butter and 10% beeswax.
Infuse 10 grams of the dried hypericum perforatum flowers in 100 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 hours at room temperature.
Steep the infused oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours and then filter it through a cheesecloth.
Mix 15 grams of the filtered oil with 85 grams of base cream and 10 grams of vitamin e oil at room temperature.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes and then pour it into a container for storage and later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve blood circulation. The extract from the Ginkgo biloba plant has been shown to reduce swelling and puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage, promoting a smoother and more even complexion. This natural remedy can effectively alleviate eye swelling, leaving the skin looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and improving blood vessel function.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide and Ginkgolide B): Act as anti-inflammatory agents, reducing swelling and pain by inhibiting the release of inflammatory chemicals and improving blood flow to the affected area.

Glycosides (specifically Ginkgo flavone glycosides): Possess antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds, which help reduce inflammation and swelling.
Seeds: The seeds contain ginkgotoxin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Extract 240ml of ginkgo biloba extract from 300g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves.
Combine 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius.
Mix 50g of sweet almond oil with the ginkgo biloba extract and stir well for 5 minutes.
Add 20g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir for another 3 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and store in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
